mission.title: harbor: [GC performance] The performance of v2 manifest deletion is not good in S3 environment
mission.description: [GC performance] The performance of v2 manifest deletion is not good in S3 environment. This issue affects 17+ users.

steps.title: Identify harbor analyze symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
In S3 backend environment, we found that it took about 39 seconds to delete a manifest via v2 API.

steps.title: Optimize GC configuration for S3 storage
steps.description:
Tune Harbor's garbage collection settings for better performance with S3 storage:
```bash
# Check current GC job configuration
kubectl get configmap -n harbor harbor-core -o yaml | grep -A 20 'GC'

# Trigger a GC dry-run to estimate the scope
# Use the Harbor API or web UI: Administration > Garbage Collection
curl -u admin:<HARBOR_PASSWORD> -X POST \
  'https://<harbor-host>/api/v2.0/system/gc/schedule' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"parameters":{"delete_untagged":true,"dry_run":true},"schedule":{"type":"Manual"}}'
```
For large registries on S3/MinIO with HDD storage, consider scheduling GC during low-traffic windows and upgrading to a Harbor version with improved GC parallelism.

steps.title: Confirm [GC performance] The performance of v2 manifest… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Garbage collection (GC) performance for v2 manifests on S3-compatible storage (e.g., MinIO) is poor because deletion of individual blob layers is slow on HDD-backed object storage. The root cause is that GC performs sequential delete operations for each manifest layer. Improvements include batching delete requests, parallelizing deletions, and optimizing the storage driver's delete path for S3-compatible backends.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
